hypothesis: Replacing CHROMA’s discrete thickness tokenization with a hybrid decoder that predicts materials categorically and thicknesses continuously, and training it end-to-end with a sequence-level CIE76 loss computed through the differentiable TMM, will reduce the held-out validation mean ∆E by at least 25% relative to the reported N=8 discrete baseline while maintaining ≥95% compliance with user-specified hard constraints.
